_meta.title: Intellectual Constellation — Exclusive Inclusion
_meta.description: The intellectual universe surrounding Exclusive Inclusion theory. Shows how the argument draws on 2,400 years of governance philosophy, modern organizational theory, and empirical legal scholarship.

nodes.label: Exclusive Inclusion
nodes.author: Oranburg
nodes.desc: Governance architecture produces systematic identity-based exclusion through self-perpetuating boards, unenforceable fiduciary duties, and absent stakeholder standing.

nodes.label: Sovereign Charities
nodes.author: Oranburg
nodes.desc: How elite private universities wield public power without public accountability. Companion article to ExI.

nodes.label: Governance as a Club Good
nodes.author: Oranburg
nodes.desc: Private governance is a club good — excludable and rivalrous.

nodes.label: Club Good Category Error
nodes.author: Oranburg
nodes.desc: The shareholder rights movement treats governance as public when it is actually a club good.

nodes.label: Beyond the Ivory Tower
nodes.author: Oranburg
nodes.desc: Virtue ethics and the classical liberal mandate for institutional integrity.

nodes.label: Market Power & Governance Power
nodes.author: Oranburg
nodes.desc: Governance power is analytically distinct from market power.

nodes.label: ESG Unintended Consequences
nodes.author: Oranburg
nodes.desc: Mandatory ESG disclosures produce compliance without substantive reform.

nodes.label: Hansmann
nodes.author: Hansmann, Henry
nodes.desc: Voice and exit as accountability mechanisms. When both are absent, managerial autonomy becomes impunity.

nodes.label: Ostrom
nodes.author: Ostrom, Elinor
nodes.desc: Commons governance: institutions excluding affected parties produce outcomes serving insiders at outsiders' expense.

nodes.label: Suchman
nodes.author: Suchman, Mark
nodes.desc: Institutional legitimacy: distinguishing perceived propriety from structural conditions.

nodes.label: Carmichael & Hamilton
nodes.author: Carmichael & Hamilton
nodes.desc: Structural discrimination: 'established and respected forces' generating inequality without hostility.

nodes.label: Olson
nodes.author: Olson, Mancur
nodes.desc: Logic of Collective Action: free-rider problem explains why excluded groups cannot organize.

nodes.label: Buchanan
nodes.author: Buchanan, James
nodes.desc: Public choice theory: rent-seeking and institutional capture. The accountability void IS rent-seeking.

nodes.label: Tullock
nodes.author: Tullock, Gordon
nodes.desc: Government Failure: bureaucratic expansion increases rigidity without improving outcomes.

nodes.label: Aristotle
nodes.author: Aristotle
nodes.desc: Nicomachean Ethics: phronesis. Politics: constitutional forms and their corruptions. Self-perpetuating oligarchy is a corrupted governance form.

nodes.label: Aquinas
nodes.author: Thomas Aquinas
nodes.desc: Summa Theologica: natural law as participation in divine reason. Law must serve the common good — an institution serving only insiders violates natural law.

nodes.label: Locke
nodes.author: John Locke
nodes.desc: Second Treatise: government derives authority from consent. Institutions that exclude stakeholders from governance lose their legitimacy.

nodes.label: Tocqueville
nodes.author: Alexis de Tocqueville
nodes.desc: Democracy in America: voluntary associations as the engine of democratic life. Universities are Tocquevillean associations that have lost accountability.

nodes.label: Mill
nodes.author: John Stuart Mill
nodes.desc: On Liberty: tyranny of the majority. The mechanism by which democratic institutions suppress minority viewpoints.

nodes.label: Adam Smith
nodes.author: Adam Smith
nodes.desc: Theory of Moral Sentiments: the impartial spectator. Institutions need mechanisms for impartial judgment.

nodes.label: Kalven Report
nodes.author: Kalven Committee
nodes.desc: The foundational report on institutional neutrality — universities should not take collective political positions.

nodes.label: Phillips
nodes.author: Phillips, James C.
nodes.desc: Empirical analysis of ideological composition of law faculties.

nodes.label: Fulton v. Philadelphia
nodes.author: U.S. Supreme Court
nodes.desc: Religious liberty and government contracting.

nodes.label: Smithers v. St. Luke's
nodes.author: NY Court of Appeals
nodes.desc: Donor standing to enforce charitable restrictions — a model for stakeholder standing.

nodes.label: Robertson v. Princeton
nodes.author: NJ Superior Court
nodes.desc: Donor family sued Princeton for diverting restricted funds. The accountability void in action.
